items.q: How do you say 'I travel by train' in Spanish?
items.why: Students use por or con. Transport always uses EN: en coche, en avión, en autobús, en tren, en barco. Exception: a pie (on foot), a caballo (on horseback). Critical holiday/travel vocab.
Students apply English pronunciation. Spanish H is ALWAYS silent. 'Hola' is 'OH-lah', not 'HO-lah'. (The breathy sound students associate with H comes from j or g+e/i in Spanish.)

items.q: How do you describe weather during a past holiday: 'It was sunny'?
items.why: Weather backdrops in past narratives use IMPERFECT. Preterite implies a single bounded event. 'Cuando llegué, hacía sol' = ongoing weather; llegué = completed action. Standard pattern for holiday writing.
It's Window. “Door” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.
The answer examiners want is: Schoolbag. “Pencil case” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: How many classroom furniture items in this lesson?
items.why: Exact answer: 6. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
